title: EXECUTIVE INTERNSHIP PROGRAMME | Blue Education
main_content:
EXECUTIVE INTERNSHIP PROGRAMME

Our organisation is always supportive of international student graduates. However, how ready are you to enter into the workforce?

Our internship programme prepares you with the skills, knowledge and multi-dimensional thinking approaches necessary to gain a head start in today's dynamic and challenging workplace.

The basis of our programme is to provide you with:
- an understanding of the Australian Qualifications Framework.
- a platform conducive for you to integrate into the Australian work culture.
- an understanding of our international processes, systems and organisational policies to be effective at the workplace.

If you are interested to participate in this internship programme, send to us your CV with a short summary of your qualifications and career aspirations. Places are limited and we only run this programme twice a year. Undergraduate and post-graduate students in business/ commerce studies are also encouraged to apply.

[Submit CV Button]

title: FEES AND CHARGES | Blue Education
main_content:
FEES AND CHARGES

OUR INITIAL ASSESSMENT

Our fees will be based on your background, circumstances and request for service support. However, our initial assessment on your request will be complimentary. Do inquire with us.

Our fees vary and will depend on the:
- type of visa application;
- amount of time estimated to prepare your application;
- extent of services you require;
- number of dependents; and
- complexity of your case.

SERVICES AND FEES

Before you officially appoint us, we will provide you a quote, or fee estimate. The estimate of charges will include:
- Professional fees;
- Disbursements (at cost, such as visa application charges, health cover, bank charges etc.); and
- Miscellaneous charges (where applicable, credit card charges, fee for urgent cases and other ancillary support etc).

Student Visa application fees payable to the Department of Home Affairs:
- $620 for Main Applicant (all ages)
- $405 for Additional Applicant Charge (18 and over)
- $135 for Additional Applicant Charge (under 18)
- $700 for Subsequent Temporary Application Charge (STAC).

STAC is charged based on the history of each applicant's visa (regardless of an extension of study period). More information about the STAC can be provided HERE.

The Fee Calculator is a useful reference.

Other costs that you would be required to budget for include:
- Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) in which you can get an estimate or purchase the policy HERE
- Medical Examination required by the Department of Home Affairs, costing around $240-$380, depending on the type of medical examination that is required (see price list HERE)

If you wish for other support services from our team, such as 24/7 emergency help, document translation, airport transfers, short-tour programmes, do connect with us.

If you would like us to assist with find you a place to stay, do let us know and you can provide your information to us HERE.

CONTACT US

title: GRADUATE / POST STUDY WORK VISA | Blue Education
main_content:
GRADUATE/ POST STUDY WORK VISA

The Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) lets you live, study and work in Australia temporarily after you have finished your studies. Students are only able to access the Temporary Graduate visa (subclass 485) once as a primary applicant.

This visa has two streams:

Graduate Work stream - for international students with an eligible qualification who graduate with skills and qualifications that relate to an occupation on the Skilled Occupation List (SOL). A visa in this stream is granted for 18 months from the date of grant.

Post-Study Work stream - for international students who graduate with a higher education degree from an Australian education provider, regardless of their field of study. This stream is only available to students who applied for and was granted, their first student visa to Australia on or after 5 November 2011. A visa in this stream can be granted for up to 4 years from the date the visa is granted, depending on the visa applicant's studies in Australia.

It is important to note that, in order to qualify for the Graduate Work Stream, you have to ensure that a skill assessment was applied for at the time of application for the 485 visa.

Book a consultation session with one of our experienced registered migration agents to find out more. We can schedule an online consultation session even if you are based offshore.

Important Conditions for the Temporary Graduate visa sub-class 485

English Language Ability
Having achieved an overall score of IELTS at least 6 with a minimum a score of 5 in each of the four test components (i.e. speaking, reading, listening and writing), undertaken in the 3 years immediately prior to lodging the visa application.

Australian Study Requirement
You meet this requirement if you can satisfy us that you have completed one or more degrees, diplomas or trade qualifications for award by an CRICOS Registered Australian educational institution for which all instruction was conducted in English, that were completed as a result of total of at least two academic years (92 weeks) study, that were completed in a total of at least 16 calendar months that have been undertaken while you were in Australia as the holder of a visa authorising you to study. English language proficiency courses or enabling programs cannot be used to meet the Australian study requirement.

Specific Requirements - Graduate Work Stream
Requirements for this visa include that:
- you nominate an occupation on the Skilled Occupation List.
- your visa application included evidence that you had applied for a skills assessment for your nominated skilled occupation by a relevant assessing authority.
- your skills have been assessed by a relevant assessing authority as suitable for your nominated skilled occupation ('suitable skills assessment').
- if your skills were assessed on the basis of a qualification obtained in Australia while you held a student visa, the qualification must have been obtained as a result of studying a CRICOS-registered course.
- you have met the Australian study requirement in the past 6 months.
- each degree, diploma or trade qualification used to meet the Australian study requirement is closely related to your nominated skilled occupation.

Specific Requirements - Post Study Work Stream
Requirements for this visa include that:
- you applied for, and were granted, your first student visa to Australia on or after 5 November 2011.
- you hold an eligible qualification.
- your qualification was conferred or awarded by an Australian university or non-university education provider that is registered on the CRICOS and that provider offers courses at degree level and above.
- your study for the qualification(s) satisfied the Australian study requirement in the past six months.

Source: https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/

We have provided highlights of the visa above, but comprehensive details are provided in the Department of Home Affairs website.

[I want to speak to a MARA Agent Button]

title: HOMESTAY | Blue Education
main_content:
HOMESTAY

If you are considering coming to Australia and would like to immerse into the Australian-way as quickly as you can, then you should choose to live with a homestay family.

Homestay is perfect for those with limited time and budget but still want to maximise the experience of living as a local in a foreign country. Your homestay family will provide your meals and a place to stay, whilst you get busy with school and your new circle of friends. More importantly, you will be treated as part of the family and of course, you'd be expected to do your part in helping around the house with the chores too!

[I WANT TO APPLY Button]

WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW ABOUT HOMESTAY

From the perspective of International Student

What are some of the challenges that an international student face in a foreign country? Language barrier, cultural adaptation, homesickness etc.

These are some of the benefits of being part of the homestay programme:
- Get acquainted with the locals through your Australian family. It can be a weekend outing, or a run to the supermarket.
- Your homestay family gives you a safe environment to learn the language. You can make mistakes and your family will correct you.
- You don't have to worry about paying all sorts of bills, like electricity, water, gas or internet connection.
- You don't have to worry about what to eat or how to prepare your meals. You can enjoy a warm meal in the comfort of your home.
- Gain first-hand experience in cultural awareness and exchange. You will find that it is a very different experience from what you've read about.

From the perspective of Homestay Host

Inviting a stranger into your home can be a daunting step. However, you can be pleasantly surprised with the rewards:
- Experience cultural exchange and learn about your foreign student's home, background, way of living and language.
- Appreciate your own culture and way of living as you promote that to an international student who may have a different lifestyle.
- Fill in the emptiness or quietness of your home especially when your kids are away at university, and you have extra rooms.
- You can make a little extra cash on the side if you have a spare room in your home and would like to help a student.
- You can build long lasting relationships through being a great host. Who knows, you can visit your next holiday destination for free!

Expectations of the Student

When you live with a homestay family, you will be treated as a member of the family. Therefore, it is important for you to:
- Greet one another in the mornings or when either party returns home from work or from school.
- Help with the basic house chores, like doing the dishes or taking the thrash out.
- Do your own laundry, clean your own room and maintain a clean bathroom and other commonly shared areas.
- Help prepare meals in the kitchen by participating in simple tasks such as cutting vegetables or cleaning up after meals.
- Notify or seek approval from your host in advance on matters affecting prior commitments with your family.
- Seek permission from your host if you intend to bring a friend home, or if you intend to add a large item in the room.
- Let your host know immediately if you have broken something or have run into trouble.
- Be considerate at all times and be conscious of house-rules, for e.g. learn to be water-wise and be aware of electricity usage.

If you are unsure of anything, it is always best to ask, rather than remain quiet.

Remember, respect, communication and understanding will help both parties go a long way.

Expectations of the Homestay Host

Am I eligible to be a host family?

We welcome traditional families, non-traditional families and single individuals who are willing to share their home with an international student. Host families are expected to:
- Provide a clean and comfortable bedroom with appropriate heating or ventilation. The bedroom should include a bed, wardrobe, desk and lamp.
- Provide 2 meals a day on weekdays and 3 meals on weekends.
- Use English as your primary language of communicate at home. You are encouraged to help your student improve their English.
- Assist your student in settling into the new environment, providing a comfortable and safe platform for them to learn.
- Include your student in the daily routines at home through conversations, support and other relevant activities.
- Help your student to organise some basic but important activities such as setting up a bank account, obtaining a local SIM card etc.
- Swiftly respond to your student in case of an emergency requiring medical or dental attention.
- Consent to obtaining the Australian Federal Police Clearance and Working with Children Certificate (if student is under 18).
- Provide all relevant documentation required for us to review your suitability to become a host family.

title: HOST EMPLOYER | Blue Education
main_content:
HOST EMPLOYER

What is the Employability Booster Programme

The EBP is a continuous support service, extended to our student graduates with qualification obtained from an Australian institution. The programme is also suitable for those who are looking at career change or who require guidance in the job marketplace.

The EBP takes our candidates through 6 stages, from developing an employer-worthy profile, to learning the cultural nuances in job applications and interview strategies.

[LEARN MORE Button]

BENEFITS TO THE HOST COMPANY

Whilst the objective is to prepare the candidate to be 'work-ready', the employer benefits from this arrangement as well. There is no obligatory to hire after the internship period.

With less pressure, the intern can quickly assimilate, contribute and be productive without involving the time and effort to go through the hiring process.

[LEARN MORE Button]

Why would the organisation be interested in the role of a Host Employer?

Test-drive 'talent'
Allow the employer to 'test-drive' the talent so that there is a 'safe period' for both parties to assess suitability.

Work Satisfaction
Increase employee satisfaction and retention rate as interns would typically have a grace period to adjust before committing fully.

Enhance Creativity
Enhance creativity with the introduction of fresh ideas and perspectives. Interns are typically eager to contribute and impress.

Early Exposure for Adaptability
Allowing graduates the earliest opportunity to work towards meeting work standards so that the industry expectations are met.

What are our roles?

Employer
Provide a safe and conducive environment for the intern to learn and contribute towards productivity.

The infrastructure should include:
- Dedicated mentor and/or supervisor to monitor performance of the intern.
- Agreed upon learning objectives and expected tasks of the intern.
- Orientation of workplace practices and day-to-day responsibilities.
- Feedback on progress and performance review of intern.
- Commitment of 3 months or, at least 16 hours a week (for no more than 8 weeks) to host the intern.
- Provision of adequate resources, equipment, and facilities to support the learning objectives.

Blue Education
Ensure a symbiotic relationship exists between both the Host Employer and Intern.

Blue Education will:
- Carry out the matching process between the Intern and the role expectation of the Host Employer.
- Clearly establish the objectives of both parties for clarity and delivery.
- Walk through the task expectations of the Intern at the workplace with the mentor/ supervisor.
- Establish the performance review framework for both parties to provide feedback on the internship arrangement.
- Provide a platform for both parties to escalate and resolve any issues arising from the internship arrangement.

Intern
Appreciate the opportunity provided by the Host Employer and contribute towards organisational productivity.

The Intern should ensure:
- Clarity of learning objectives and task expectations set.
- Commitment of 3 months or, at least 16 hours a week (for no more than 8 weeks) in the internship programme.
- On-time reporting at the workplace as per agreed upon schedule.
- Receive formal feedback from mentor and/ or supervisor assigned on areas for improvement.
- Application and extension of the knowledge gained in class and learning curriculum.

title: MIGRATION | Blue Education
main_content:
MIGRATION SERVICES

Registered Migration Agents & Immigration Lawyer Support in Australia

Blue Education provides professional Australian immigration services through a team of Registered Migration Agents, specialising in Skilled Migration, Employer Sponsored Visas, Family Migration, and Business Migration.

We also work in collaboration with experienced Immigration Lawyers and Migration Lawyers for complex matters such as visa refusals, cancellations, and appeals - ensuring you receive the right level of expertise at every stage of your migration journey.

Trusted Australian Migration Advice You Can Rely On

We provide efficient and reliable consultation to give an accurate and honest assessment of your chances for migration to Australia, and subsequently help you identify the best visa options that you may consider as we take you through the application process hand-in-hand.

Migrating to Australia is a significant decision. Our role is to provide accurate, honest, and transparent migration advice, helping you understand:
- Your eligibility for Australian visas
- Your realistic chances of success
- The most suitable visa options based on your profile
- Clear timelines, requirements, and expectations

From your initial assessment through to visa grant, our team supports you step-by-step, ensuring your application is professionally prepared and lodged in accordance with the requirements.

Book a Consultation with a Registered Migration Agent

Book a consultation with one of our Registered Migration Agents in Australia to discuss your migration options and next steps.

We offer online consultations via Google Meet, Skype, or LINE, making it easy to access professional migration advice regardless of where you are in the world.

Once you appoint one of our Registered Migration Agents as your authorised representative, you will receive:
- Full transparency throughout the visa process
- Clear communication and regular updates
- Professional management from preliminary assessment to final decision

Types of Australian Visa & Migration Services and Support

Skilled & Work Migration:
- Skilled Independent & State Nominated Visas
- Employer Sponsored Visas
- Sponsored Skilled Visas
- Graduate Visa
- Post-Study Work Visa

Employer Sponsored Migration:
- Employer Nomination Scheme (ENS)
- Temporary Skill Shortage (TSS) Visa
- Labour Agreement Support

Family & Partner Migration:
- Partner / Spouse / De-Facto Visas
- Child Visas
- Family Migration Pathways

Business & Investment Migration:
- Business Innovation & Investment Visas
- Entrepreneur & Investor Pathways

Visa Refusals & Appeals:
- Visa Refusals
- Visa Cancellations
- Administrative Appeals

Visa Fees & Government Charges

Australian visa application charges are set by the Department of Home Affairs and vary depending on visa type and applicant circumstances.

You may estimate your Visa Application Charge (VAC) using the official Visa Pricing Calculator available on the Department's website.

Our professional fees depend on the level of assistance required.

Why Choose Blue Education for Migration Services:
- Licensed / Registered Migration Agents in Australia
- Honest, realistic eligibility assessments
- End-to-end migration support
- Online consultations available worldwide
- Immigration Lawyer support for complex cases

Book your migration consultation today and take the first step toward your Australian migration pathway.

[I want to speak to a MARA Agent Button]

title: Blue Education - Australian Education & Migration Specialist (OSHC-OVC)
main_content:
Overseas Student Health Cover

OSHC or the Overseas Student Health Cover provides insurance protection for an international student whilst studying in Australia. As a minimum, the cover would provide for selected cost of medical consult, hospital treatment, ambulance and limited pharmaceuticals. You will not be able to lodge a student visa application without the appropriate cover. If you are coming with your dependents i.e. your partner/ spouse and children, you are required to purchase a couple's cover or family cover.

You can get a cover through any of our partners below, and rest assured that we will be able to obtain your OSHC Certificate promptly for your visa lodgment.

Partners:
- OSHC Australia [Get a Quote]
- Annalink OSHC-Students [Get a Quote]
- Konpare [Get a Quote]

Overseas Visitors Cover (OVC)

For students and family members who may not be eligible for OSHC, we can also assist with arranging Overseas Visitors Cover (OVC) through our partner providers.

Partners:
- Allianz Care [LEARN MORE]
- Annalink OVHC [LEARN MORE]
- Konpare OVHC [LEARN MORE]

title: FAMILY & PARTNER VISA | Blue Education
main_content:
FAMILY & PARTNER VISA

Business, Partner and Family visa applications can be strenuous, time-consuming and a huge undertaking. If you are looking for a migration agent with extensive experience in this area, contact us.

Book your FREE consultation session with one of our Registered Migration Agent for a free assessment and find out more about how we can assist you to navigate through the visa application process.

Family and Partner Migration

Being separated from your partner and family members can be a frustrating experience. Australia has a number of visas available to enable an Australian (or an Australian Permanent Resident) to sponsor partner, child and family member.

The family and partner migration schemes allow eligible persons to sponsor their partner or family members to join them in Australia on a temporary or permanent basis.

Both the sponsor and applicant will need to meet the eligibility criteria which varies depending on the type of visa one qualifies for.

The following Visa streams are some which are available:

Partner Category Visa Options

If you wish to bring your partner to Australia, there are different types of partner visas available. You may be eligible if you are:
- Spouse of an Australian citizen, Australian permanent resident or eligible New Zealand citizen, or
- Fiance of an Australian citizen, Australian permanent resident or eligible New Zealand citizen; or
- De facto partner (including same-sex relationships) with an Australian citizen, Australian permanent resident or eligible New Zealand citizen

Child and Adoption Visa Options

This visa application typically includes:
- Children with parents residing in Australia.
- Persons under 18 years of age (and if a person is 18 years of age or older, he/she must be single and financially dependent on their parent).
- Single persons (not married, engaged to be married, or in a de facto relationship).

Parent Category Visa Options

This visa application typically includes:
- Parents with 1 or more children residing in Australia.
